NKJV.1: Jesus spoke these words, lifted up His eyes to heaven, and said: “Father,  the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son also may glorify You,
NKJV.2: as You have given Him authority over all flesh, that He should give eternal life to as many  as You have given Him.
NKJV.3: And  this is eternal life, that they may know You,  the only true God, and Jesus Christ  whom You have sent.
NKJV.4: I have glorified You on the earth.  I have finished the work  which You have given Me to do.
NKJV.5: And now, O Father, glorify Me together with Yourself, with the glory  which I had with You before the world was.
NKJV.6: “I have manifested Your name to the men  whom You have given Me out of the world.  They were Yours, You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.
NKJV.7: Now they have known that all things which You have given Me are from You.
NKJV.8: For I have given to them the words which You have given Me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came forth from You; and they have believed that You sent Me.
NKJV.9: “I pray for them.  I do not pray for the world but for those whom You have given Me, for they are Yours.
NKJV.10: And all Mine are Yours, and  Yours are Mine, and I am glorified in them.
NKJV.11: Now I am no longer in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to You. Holy Father, keep through Your name those whom You have given Me, that they may be one as We are.
NKJV.12: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in Your name. Those whom You gave Me I have kept; and  none of them is lost  except the son of perdition,  that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
NKJV.13: But now I come to You, and these things I speak in the world, that they may have My joy fulfilled in themselves.
NKJV.14: I have given them Your word;  and the world has hated them because they are not of the world,  just as I am not of the world.
NKJV.15: I do not pray that You should take them out of the world, but  that You should keep them from the evil one.
NKJV.16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
NKJV.17: Sanctify them by Your truth.  Your word is truth.
NKJV.18: As You sent Me into the world, I also have sent them into the world.
NKJV.19: And  for their sakes I sanctify Myself, that they also may be sanctified by the truth.
NKJV.20: “I do not pray for these alone, but also for those who will believe in Me through their word;
NKJV.21: that they all may be one, as You, Father, are in Me, and I in You; that they also may be one in Us, that the world may believe that You sent Me.
NKJV.22: And the  glory which You gave Me I have given them,  that they may be one just as We are one:
NKJV.23: I in them, and You in Me;  that they may be made perfect in one, and that the world may know that You have sent Me, and have loved them as You have loved Me.
NKJV.24: “Father, I desire that they also whom You gave Me may be with Me where I am, that they may behold My glory which You have given Me;  for You loved Me before the foundation of the world.
NKJV.25: O righteous Father!  The world has not known You, but  I have known You; and  these have known that You sent Me.
NKJV.26: And I have declared to them Your name, and will declare it, that the love with which You loved Me may be in them, and I in them.”

ESV.1: When Jesus had spoken these words, he lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son that the Son may glorify you,
ESV.2: since you have given him authority over all flesh, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
ESV.3: And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
ESV.4: I glorified you on earth, having accomplished the work that you gave me to do.
ESV.5: And now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory that I had with you before the world existed.
ESV.6: “I have manifested your name to the people whom you gave me out of the world. Yours they were, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
ESV.7: Now they know that everything that you have given me is from you.
ESV.8: For I have given them the words that you gave me, and they have received them and have come to know in truth that I came from you; and they have believed that you sent me.
ESV.9: I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.
ESV.10: All mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.
ESV.11: And I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them in your name, which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are one.
ESV.12: While I was with them, I kept them in your name, which you have given me. I have guarded them, and not one of them has been lost except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
ESV.13: But now I am coming to you, and these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
ESV.14: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
ESV.15: I do not ask that you take them out of the world, but that you keep them from the evil one.
ESV.16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
ESV.17: Sanctify them  in the truth; your word is truth.
ESV.18: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
ESV.19: And for their sake I consecrate myself,  that they also may be sanctified  in truth.
ESV.20: “I do not ask for these only, but also for those who will believe in me through their word,
ESV.21: that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
ESV.22: The glory that you have given me I have given to them, that they may be one even as we are one,
ESV.23: I in them and you in me, that they may become perfectly one, so that the world may know that you sent me and loved them even as you loved me.
ESV.24: Father, I desire that they also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory that you have given me because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
ESV.25: O righteous Father, even though the world does not know you, I know you, and these know that you have sent me.
ESV.26: I made known to them your name, and I will continue to make it known, that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

NASB.1: Jesus spoke these things; and lifting up His eyes to heaven, He said, 'Father, the hour has come; glorify Your Son, that the Son may glorify You,
NASB.2: even as You gave Him authority over all flesh, that to all whom You have given Him, He may give eternal life.
NASB.3: 'This is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom You have sent.
NASB.4: 'I glorified You on the earth, having accomplished the work which You have given Me to do.
NASB.5: 'Now, Father, glorify Me together with Yourself, with the glory which I had with You before the world was.
NASB.6: 'I have manifested Your name to the men whom You gave Me out of the world; they were Yours and You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.
NASB.7: 'Now they have come to know that everything You have given Me is from You;
NASB.8: for the words which You gave Me I have given to them; and they received [them] and truly understood that I came forth from You, and they believed that You sent Me.
NASB.9: 'I ask on their behalf; I do not ask on behalf of the world, but of those whom You have given Me; for they are Yours;
NASB.10: and all things that are Mine are Yours, and Yours are Mine; and I have been glorified in them.
NASB.11: 'I am no longer in the world; and [yet] they themselves are in the world, and I come to You. Holy Father, keep them in Your name, [the name] which You have given Me, that they may be one even as We [are].
NASB.12: 'While I was with them, I was keeping them in Your name which You have given Me; and I guarded them and not one of them perished but the son of perdition, so that the Scripture would be fulfilled.
NASB.13: 'But now I come to You; and these things I speak in the world so that they may have My joy made full in themselves.
NASB.14: 'I have given them Your word; and the world has hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
NASB.15: 'I do not ask You to take them out of the world, but to keep them from the evil [one].
NASB.16: 'They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
NASB.17: 'Sanctify them in the truth; Your word is truth.
NASB.18: 'As You sent Me into the world, I also have sent them into the world.
NASB.19: 'For their sakes I sanctify Myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.
NASB.20: 'I do not ask on behalf of these alone, but for those also who believe in Me through their word;
NASB.21: that they may all be one; even as You, Father, [are] in Me and I in You, that they also may be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.
NASB.22: 'The glory which You have given Me I have given to them, that they may be one, just as We are one;
NASB.23: I in them and You in Me, that they may be perfected in unity, so that the world may know that You sent Me, and loved them, even as You have loved Me.
NASB.24: 'Father, I desire that they also, whom You have given Me, be with Me where I am, so that they may see My glory which You have given Me, for You loved Me before the foundation of the world.
NASB.25: 'O righteous Father, although the world has not known You, yet I have known You; and these have known that You sent Me;
NASB.26: and I have made Your name known to them, and will make it known, so that the love with which You loved Me may be in them, and I in them.'

NLT.1: After saying all these things, Jesus looked up to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son so he can give glory back to you.
NLT.2: For you have given him authority over everyone. He gives eternal life to each one you have given him.
NLT.3: And this is the way to have eternal life — to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, the one you sent to earth.
NLT.4: I brought glory to you here on earth by completing the work you gave me to do.
NLT.5: Now, Father, bring me into the glory we shared before the world began.
NLT.6: “I have revealed you to the ones you gave me from this world. They were always yours. You gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
NLT.7: Now they know that everything I have is a gift from you,
NLT.8: for I have passed on to them the message you gave me. They accepted it and know that I came from you, and they believe you sent me.
NLT.9: “My prayer is not for the world, but for those you have given me, because they belong to you.
NLT.10: All who are mine belong to you, and you have given them to me, so they bring me glory.
NLT.11: Now I am departing from the world; they are staying in this world, but I am coming to you. Holy Father, you have given me your name; now protect them by the power of your name so that they will be united just as we are.
NLT.12: During my time here, I protected them by the power of the name you gave me. I guarded them so that not one was lost, except the one headed for destruction, as the Scriptures foretold.
NLT.13: “Now I am coming to you. I told them many things while I was with them in this world so they would be filled with my joy.
NLT.14: I have given them your word. And the world hates them because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
NLT.15: I’m not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them safe from the evil one.
NLT.16: They do not belong to this world any more than I do.
NLT.17: Make them holy by your truth; teach them your word, which is truth.
NLT.18: Just as you sent me into the world, I am sending them into the world.
NLT.19: And I give myself as a holy sacrifice for them so they can be made holy by your truth.
NLT.20: “I am praying not only for these disciples but also for all who will ever believe in me through their message.
NLT.21: I pray that they will all be one, just as you and I are one — as you are in me, Father, and I am in you. And may they be in us so that the world will believe you sent me.
NLT.22: “I have given them the glory you gave me, so they may be one as we are one.
NLT.23: I am in them and you are in me. May they experience such perfect unity that the world will know that you sent me and that you love them as much as you love me.
NLT.24: Father, I want these whom you have given me to be with me where I am. Then they can see all the glory you gave me because you loved me even before the world began!
NLT.25: “O righteous Father, the world doesn’t know you, but I do; and these disciples know you sent me.
NLT.26: I have revealed you to them, and I will continue to do so. Then your love for me will be in them, and I will be in them.”

WEB.1: Jesus said these things, and lifting up his eyes to heaven, he said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may also glorify you;
WEB.2: even as you gave him authority over all flesh, he will give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
WEB.3: This is eternal life, that they should know you, the only true God, and him whom you sent, Jesus Christ.
WEB.4: I glorified you on the earth. I have accomplished the work which you have given me to do.
WEB.5: Now, Father, glorify me with your own self with the glory which I had with you before the world existed.
WEB.6: I revealed your name to the people whom you have given me out of the world. They were yours, and you have given them to me. They have kept your word.
WEB.7: Now they have known that all things whatever you have given me are from you,
WEB.8: for the words which you have given me I have given to them, and they received them, and knew for sure that I came from you, and they have believed that you sent me.
WEB.9: I pray for them. I don’t pray for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.
WEB.10: All things that are mine are yours, and yours are mine, and I am glorified in them.
WEB.11: I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them through your name which you have given me, that they may be one, even as we are.
WEB.12: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in your name. Those whom you have given me I have kept. None of them is lost, except the son of destruction, that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
WEB.13: But now I come to you, and I say these things in the world, that they may have my joy made full in themselves.
WEB.14: I have given them your word. The world hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
WEB.15: I pray not that you would take them from the world, but that you would keep them from the evil one.
WEB.16: They are not of the world even as I am not of the world.
WEB.17: Sanctify them in your truth. Your word is truth.
WEB.18: As you sent me into the world, even so I have sent them into the world.
WEB.19: For their sakes I sanctify myself, that they themselves also may be sanctified in truth.
WEB.20: Not for these only do I pray, but for those also who believe in me through their word,
WEB.21: that they may all be one; even as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be one in us; that the world may believe that you sent me.
WEB.22: The glory which you have given me, I have given to them; that they may be one, even as we are one;
WEB.23: I in them, and you in me, that they may be perfected into one; that the world may know that you sent me, and loved them, even as you loved me.
WEB.24: Father, I desire that they also whom you have given me be with me where I am, that they may see my glory, which you have given me, for you loved me before the foundation of the world.
WEB.25: Righteous Father, the world hasn’t known you, but I knew you; and these knew that you sent me.
WEB.26: I made known to them your name, and will make it known; that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

CSB17.1: Jesus spoke these things, looked up to heaven, and said: “Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son so that the Son <sup>ⓚ</sup>may glorify you, <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.2: since you gave him authority over all flesh, <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓜ</sup>so that he may give eternal life <sup>ⓝ</sup>to everyone you have given him. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.3: This is eternal life: that they may know you, the only <sup>ⓑ</sup>true <sup>ⓒ</sup>God, and the one you have sent <sup>ⓓ</sup>— Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: I have glorified you on the earth by completing the work you gave me to do.
CSB17.5: Now, Father, <sup>ⓕ</sup>glorify me in your presence with that glory <sup>ⓖ</sup>I had with you before the world existed. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.6: “I have revealed your name <sup>ⓘ</sup> to the people you gave me <sup>ⓙ</sup> from the world. <sup>ⓚ</sup> They were yours, you gave them to me, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and they have kept your word. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.7: Now they know that everything you have given is from you,
CSB17.8: because I have given them the words you gave me. <sup>ⓝ</sup> They have received them and have known for certain that I came from you. <sup>ⓐ</sup> They have believed that you sent me.
CSB17.9: “I pray <sup>[2]</sup> for them. I am not praying for the world but for those you have given me, <sup>ⓑ</sup> because they are yours.
CSB17.10: Everything I have is yours, and everything you have is mine, <sup>ⓒ</sup>and I am glorified in them.
CSB17.11: I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. <sup>ⓓ</sup>Holy <sup>ⓔ</sup>Father, protect <sup>[3]</sup>them by your name <sup>ⓕ</sup>that you have given me, so that they may be one <sup>ⓖ</sup> as we are <sup>ⓗ</sup>one.
CSB17.12: While I was with them, I was protecting them by your name that you have given me. I guarded <sup>ⓘ</sup>them and not one of them is lost, except the son of destruction, <sup>[4]</sup>so that the Scripture <sup>ⓙ</sup>may be fulfilled. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.13: Now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy completed in them.
CSB17.14: I have given them your word. The world hated <sup>ⓛ</sup>them because they are not of the world, <sup>ⓜ</sup>just as I am not of the world. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.15: I am not praying that you take them out of the world but that you protect them from the evil one. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.17: Sanctify <sup>ⓒ</sup> them by the truth;  <sup>ⓓ</sup>your word is truth.
CSB17.18: As you sent me into the world, <sup>ⓔ</sup>I also have sent them into the world.
CSB17.19: I sanctify myself for them, so that they also may be sanctified by the truth.
CSB17.20: “I pray not only for these, but also for those who believe in me through their word.
CSB17.21: May they all be one, <sup>ⓕ</sup>as you, Father, are in me and I am in you. <sup>ⓖ</sup>May they also be <sup>[5]</sup>in us, so that the world may believe you sent me.
CSB17.22: I have given them the glory <sup>ⓗ</sup>you have given me, so that they may be one as we are one.
CSB17.23: I am in them and you are in me, <sup>ⓘ</sup> so that they may be made completely one, that the world may know you have sent me and have loved <sup>ⓙ</sup>them as you have loved me. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.24: “Father, I want those you have given me to be with me where I am, <sup>ⓛ</sup> so that they will see my glory, which you have given me because you loved me before the world’s foundation.
CSB17.25: Righteous Father, the world has not known you. However, I have known you, <sup>ⓜ</sup>and they have known that you sent me.
CSB17.26: I made your name <sup>ⓝ</sup>known to them and will continue to make it known, so that the love you have loved me with may be in them and I may be in them.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>

AMP.1: When Jesus had spoken these things, He raised His eyes to heaven [in prayer] and said, "Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, so that Your Son may glorify You.
AMP.2: Just as You have given Him power and authority over all mankind, [now glorify Him] so that He may give eternal life to all whom You have given Him [to be His—permanently and forever].
AMP.3: Now this is eternal life: that they may know You, the only true [supreme and sovereign] God, and [in the same manner know] Jesus [as the] Christ whom You have sent.
AMP.4: I have glorified You [down here] on the earth by completing the work that You gave Me to do.
AMP.5: Now, Father, glorify Me together with Yourself, with the glory and majesty that I had with You before the world existed.
AMP.6: "I have manifested Your name [and revealed Your very self, Your real self] to the people whom You have given Me out of the world; they were Yours and You gave them to Me, and they have kept and obeyed Your word.
AMP.7: Now [at last] they know [with confident assurance] that all You have given Me is from You [it is really and truly Yours].
AMP.8: For the words which You gave Me I have given them; and they received and accepted them and truly understood [with confident assurance] that I came from You [from Your presence], and they believed [without any doubt] that You sent Me.
AMP.9: I pray for them; I do not pray for the world, but for those You have given Me, because they belong to You;
AMP.10: and all things that are Mine are Yours, and [all things that are] Yours are Mine; and I am glorified in them.
AMP.11: I am no longer in the world; yet they are still in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father, keep them in Your name, the name which You have given Me, so that they may be one just as We are.
AMP.12: While I was with them, I was keeping them in Your name which You have given Me; and I guarded them and protected them, and not one of them was lost except the son of destruction, so that the Scripture would be fulfilled.
AMP.13: But now I am coming to You; and I say these things [while I am still] in the world so that they may experience My joy made full and complete and perfect within them [filling their hearts with My delight].
AMP.14: I have given to them Your word [the message You gave Me]; and the world has hated them because they are not of the world and do not belong to the world, just as I am not of the world and do not belong to it.
AMP.15: I do not ask You to take them out of the world, but that You keep them and protect them from the evil one.
AMP.16: They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
AMP.17: Sanctify them in the truth [set them apart for Your purposes, make them holy]; Your word is truth.
AMP.18: Just as You commissioned and sent Me into the world, I also have commissioned and sent them (believers) into the world.
AMP.19: For their sake I sanctify Myself [to do Your will], so that they also may be sanctified [set apart, dedicated, made holy] in [Your] truth.
AMP.20: "I do not pray for these alone [it is not for their sake only that I make this request], but also for [all] those who [will ever] believe and trust in Me through their message,
AMP.21: that they all may be one; just as You, Father, are in Me and I in You, that they also may be one in Us, so that the world may believe [without any doubt] that You sent Me.
AMP.22: I have given to them the glory and honor which You have given Me, that they may be one, just as We are one;
AMP.23: I in them and You in Me, that they may be perfected and completed into one, so that the world may know [without any doubt] that You sent Me, and [that You] have loved them, just as You have loved Me.
AMP.24: Father, I desire that they also, whom You have given to Me [as Your gift to Me], may be with Me where I am, so that they may see My glory which You have given Me, because You loved Me before the foundation of the world.
AMP.25: "O just and righteous Father, although the world has not known You and has never acknowledged You [and the revelation of Your mercy], yet I have always known You; and these [believers] know [without any doubt] that You sent Me;
AMP.26: and I have made Your name known to them, and will continue to make it known, so that the love with which You have loved Me may be in them [overwhelming their heart], and I [may be] in them."

MSG.1: Jesus said these things. Then, raising his eyes in prayer, he said: Father, it's time. Display the bright splendor of your Son So the Son in turn may show your bright splendor.
MSG.2: You put him in charge of everything human So he might give real and eternal life to all in his charge.
MSG.3: And this is the real and eternal life: That they know you, The one and only true God, And Jesus Christ, whom you sent.
MSG.4: I glorified you on earth By completing down to the last detail What you assigned me to do.
MSG.5: And now, Father, glorify me with your very own splendor, The very splendor I had in your presence Before there was a world.
MSG.6: I spelled out your character in detail To the men and women you gave me. They were yours in the first place; Then you gave them to me, And they have now done what you said.
MSG.7: They know now, beyond the shadow of a doubt, That everything you gave me is firsthand from you,
MSG.8: For the message you gave me, I gave them; And they took it, and were convinced That I came from you. They believed that you sent me.
MSG.9: I pray for them. I'm not praying for the God-rejecting world But for those you gave me, For they are yours by right.
MSG.10: Everything mine is yours, and yours mine, And my life is on display in them.
MSG.11: For I'm no longer going to be visible in the world; They'll continue in the world While I return to you. Holy Father, guard them as they pursue this life That you conferred as a gift through me, So they can be one heart and mind
MSG.12: As we are one heart and mind. As long as I was with them, I guarded them In the pursuit of the life you gave through me; I even posted a night watch. And not one of them got away, Except for the rebel bent on destruction (the exception that proved the rule of Scripture).
MSG.13: Now I'm returning to you. I'm saying these things in the world's hearing So my people can experience My joy completed in them.
MSG.14: I gave them your word; The godless world hated them because of it, Because they didn't join the world's ways,
MSG.15: Just as I didn't join the world's ways. I'm not asking that you take them out of the world But that you guard them from the Evil One.
MSG.16: They are no more defined by the world Than I am defined by the world.
MSG.17: Make them holy—consecrated—with the truth; Your word is consecrating truth.
MSG.18: In the same way that you gave me a mission in the world, I give them a mission in the world.
MSG.19: I'm consecrating myself for their sakes So they'll be truth-consecrated in their mission.
MSG.20: I'm praying not only for them But also for those who will believe in me Because of them and their witness about me.
MSG.21: The goal is for all of them to become one heart and mind— Just as you, Father, are in me and I in you, So they might be one heart and mind with us. Then the world might believe that you, in fact, sent me.
MSG.22: The same glory you gave me, I gave them, So they'll be as unified and together as we are—
MSG.23: I in them and you in me. Then they'll be mature in this oneness, And give the godless world evidence That you've sent me and loved them In the same way you've loved me.
MSG.24: Father, I want those you gave me To be with me, right where I am, So they can see my glory, the splendor you gave me, Having loved me Long before there ever was a world.
MSG.25: Righteous Father, the world has never known you, But I have known you, and these disciples know That you sent me on this mission.
MSG.26: I have made your very being known to them— Who you are and what you do— And continue to make it known, So that your love for me Might be in them Exactly as I am in them.

NIV.1:
Jesus Prays for Himself
After Jesus said this, he looked toward heaven and prayed:
“Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may glorify you.
NIV.2: For you granted him authority over all people that he might give eternal life to all those you have given him.
NIV.3: Now this is eternal life: that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.
NIV.4: I have brought you glory on earth by completing the work you gave me to do.
NIV.5: And now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I had with you before the world began.
NIV.6:
Jesus Prays for His Disciples
“I have revealed you to those whom you gave me out of the world. They were yours; you gave them to me and they have obeyed your word.
NIV.7: Now they know that everything you have given me comes from you.
NIV.8: For I gave them the words you gave me and they accepted them. They knew with certainty that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
NIV.9: I pray for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those you have given me, for they are yours.
NIV.10: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine. And glory has come to me through them.
NIV.11: I will remain in the world no longer, but they are still in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them by the power of your name—the name you gave me—so that they may be one as we are one.
NIV.12: While I was with them, I protected them and kept them safe by that name you gave me. None has been lost except the one doomed to destruction so that Scripture would be fulfilled.
NIV.13: “I am coming to you now, but I say these things while I am still in the world, so that they may have the full measure of my joy within them.
NIV.14: I have given them your word and the world has hated them, for they are not of the world any more than I am of the world.
NIV.15: My prayer is not that you take them out of the world but that you protect them from the evil one.
NIV.16: They are not of the world, even as I am not of it.
NIV.17: Sanctify them by the truth; your word is truth.
NIV.18: As you sent me into the world, I have sent them into the world.
NIV.19: For them I sanctify myself, that they too may be truly sanctified.
NIV.20:
Jesus Prays for All Believers
“My prayer is not for them alone. I pray also for those who will believe in me through their message,
NIV.21: that all of them may be one, Father, just as you are in me and I am in you. May they also be in us so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
NIV.22: I have given them the glory that you gave me, that they may be one as we are one:
NIV.23: I in them and you in me. May they be brought to complete unity to let the world know that you sent me and have loved them even as you have loved me.
NIV.24: “Father, I want those you have given me to be with me where I am, and to see my glory, the glory you have given me because you loved me before the creation of the world.
NIV.25: “Righteous Father, though the world does not know you, I know you, and they know that you have sent me.
NIV.26: I have made you known to them, and will continue to make you known in order that the love you have for me may be in them and that I myself may be in them.”

NRSVCE.1: After Jesus had spoken these words, he looked up to heaven and said, “Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son so that the Son may glorify you,
NRSVCE.2: since you have given him authority over all people, to give eternal life to all whom you have given him.
NRSVCE.3: And this is eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
NRSVCE.4: I glorified you on earth by finishing the work that you gave me to do.
NRSVCE.5: So now, Father, glorify me in your own presence with the glory that I had in your presence before the world existed.
NRSVCE.6: “I have made your name known to those whom you gave me from the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
NRSVCE.7: Now they know that everything you have given me is from you;
NRSVCE.8: for the words that you gave to me I have given to them, and they have received them and know in truth that I came from you; and they have believed that you sent me.
NRSVCE.9: I am asking on their behalf; I am not asking on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those whom you gave me, because they are yours.
NRSVCE.10: All mine are yours, and yours are mine; and I have been glorified in them.
NRSVCE.11: And now I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one, as we are one.
NRSVCE.12: While I was with them, I protected them in your name that you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost except the one destined to be lost, so that the scripture might be fulfilled.
NRSVCE.13: But now I am coming to you, and I speak these things in the world so that they may have my joy made complete in themselves.
NRSVCE.14: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
NRSVCE.15: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but I ask you to protect them from the evil one.
NRSVCE.16: They do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
NRSVCE.17: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
NRSVCE.18: As you have sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
NRSVCE.19: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, so that they also may be sanctified in truth.
NRSVCE.20: “I ask not only on behalf of these, but also on behalf of those who will believe in me through their word,
NRSVCE.21: that they may all be one. As you, Father, are in me and I am in you, may they also be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
NRSVCE.22: The glory that you have given me I have given them, so that they may be one, as we are one,
NRSVCE.23: I in them and you in me, that they may become completely one, so that the world may know that you have sent me and have loved them even as you have loved me.
NRSVCE.24: Father, I desire that those also, whom you have given me, may be with me where I am, to see my glory, which you have given me because you loved me before the foundation of the world.
NRSVCE.25: “Righteous Father, the world does not know you, but I know you; and these know that you have sent me.
NRSVCE.26: I made your name known to them, and I will make it known, so that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.”

NET.1: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he looked upward to heaven and said, “Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son, so that your Son may glorify you —
NET.2: just as you have given him authority over all humanity, so that he may give eternal life to everyone you have given him.
NET.3: Now this is eternal life — that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you sent.
NET.4: I glorified you on earth by completing the work you gave me to do.
NET.5: And now, Father, glorify me at your side with the glory I had with you before the world was created.
NET.6: “I have revealed your name to the men you gave me out of the world. They belonged to you, and you gave them to me, and they have obeyed your word.
NET.7: Now they understand that everything you have given me comes from you,
NET.8: because I have given them the words you have given me. They accepted them and really understand that I came from you, and they believed that you sent me.
NET.9: I am praying on behalf of them. I am not praying on behalf of the world, but on behalf of those you have given me, because they belong to you.
NET.10: Everything I have belongs to you, and everything you have belongs to me, and I have been glorified by them.
NET.11: I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them safe in your name that you have given me, so that they may be one just as we are one.
NET.12: When I was with them I kept them safe and watched over them in your name that you have given me. Not one of them was lost except the one destined for destruction, so that the scripture could be fulfilled.
NET.13: But now I am coming to you, and I am saying these things in the world, so they may experience my joy completed in themselves.
NET.14: I have given them your word, and the world has hated them, because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
NET.15: I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but that you keep them safe from the evil one.
NET.16: They do not belong to the world just as I do not belong to the world.
NET.17: Set them apart in the truth; your word is truth.
NET.18: Just as you sent me into the world, so I sent them into the world.
NET.19: And I set myself apart on their behalf, so that they too may be truly set apart.
NET.20: “I am not praying only on their behalf, but also on behalf of those who believe in me through their testimony,
NET.21: that they will all be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I am in you. I pray that they will be in us, so that the world will believe that you sent me.
NET.22: The glory you gave to me I have given to them, that they may be one just as we are one —
NET.23: I in them and you in me — that they may be completely one, so that the world will know that you sent me, and you have loved them just as you have loved me.
NET.24: “Father, I want those you have given me to be with me where I am, so that they can see my glory that you gave me because you loved me before the creation of the world .
NET.25: Righteous Father, even if the world does not know you, I know you, and these men know that you sent me.
NET.26: I made known your name to them, and I will continue to make it known, so that the love you have loved me with may be in them, and I may be in them.”
